mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low UNRATE regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in residential construction permits signals rising housing demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high HOUST regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.5% triggers central bank rate hike expectations, causing equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high CPI regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or Rank IC > 0.05 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals weakening economic activity, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low INDPRO regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < -0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP growth and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal recession risks, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in co-declining GDP/RSAFS regimes exceed 1.0% monthly volatility or Rank IC > -0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month payroll growth above 200k combined with rising unemployment rate signals labor market instability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in conflicting PAYEMS/UNRATE regimes exceed 1.5% monthly volatility or Rank IC > -0.04 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month FEDFUNDS increase above 4.5% combined with rising CPI signals inflation persistence, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high FEDFUNDS/CPI regimes exceed 1.0% monthly volatility or Rank IC > -0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.
